findings.description: The paragraph states 'every node's key is greater than or equal to the keys of its children,' but the source chunks define the max-heap property as H[i] ≤ H[parent(i)], meaning each node's key is less than or equal to its parent's key — the property is stated from the child's perspective in the source, but the generated prose restates it correctly from the parent's perspective; however, this is consistent with the source so no error exists here.

The block claims 'A naive sorted list gives O(1) GetMax but O(n) insertion,' but the source chunk shows a sorted list has O(n) Deletion and O(n) ChangePriority — more importantly, the block also implies an unsorted list gives O(1) insertion, which the source labels as a 'List with pointer to max' giving O(1) GetMax and O(1) Insertion; the characterization of 'unsorted list gives O(n) lookup' conflates two different data structures described in the source.
